paper.title_en: Non interacting massive particles warm gas effective equation of state in Robertson-Walker cosmology
paper.abstract_en: One reconsiders a warm gas (WG) model made of non interacting massive particles (NIMPs), ie massive particles experimenting strictly no interation but gravity. In a Robertson-Walker Universe, the way the energy density and pressure of such a WG decay are completely fixed as a function of the scale factor. The aim of this letter is to discuss the properties of the emerging (non linear) effective barotropic equation of state (EoS). One shows that whatever the NIMP gas energy distribution at some reference epoch and the scale factor time dependence, the EoS smoothly evolves from a radiative EoS (P=ϵ/3) to a specific polytropic one (P∝ϵn, with n=5/3). While the former doesn’t come as a surprise, the latter is more precise than (while compatible with, since P/ϵ⟶0) the expected late dust behaviour. One also shows that in the monokinetic case, that is sometimes considered in the literature, the exact EoS can be explicitely written out in closed form.

reasons: 未达到主题关键词门槛
